meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a lack of portfolio. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional apps and win clients fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends by building a high-impact SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, you can ship production-ready, modular SaaS features in hours. This repeatable workflow replaces static samples with a live, scalable codebase that proves your engineering expertise.
content.intro:
You are staring at the abyss of a blank screen, the silence of a client who hasn't seen a single line of your code. The 'no portfolio' hurdle is the silent killer of freelance careers, turning high-potential developers into perpetual applicants. You don't need a decade of experience; you need a system that turns ideas into functional, beautiful SaaS products overnight.

We are shifting the paradigm. Instead of building toy projects, you will deploy a modular SaaS starter kit that acts as your professional engine. By combining the architectural precision of Antigravity with the raw execution power of Claude Code, you will transform from a novice into an elite builder who ships faster than the competition can write a proposal.

steps.title: Architect the Core Identity
steps.description: Define your SaaS foundation. Use Antigravity to generate a clean, scalable boilerplate that includes authentication, database schema, and UI layouts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS starter boilerplate using Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. Include user authentication, a protected dashboard layout, and a responsive navigation bar.

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Set up a robust schema that handles user data efficiently. This ensures your portfolio project looks like a professional enterprise application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application with models for Users, Organizations, and Subscriptions. Include relational integrity and timestamps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "npx prisma migrate dev --name init_saas_schema"

steps.title: Implement High-Velocity UI Components
steps.description: Populate your dashboard with functional components. Use Claude Code to inject pre-styled UI elements that demonstrate your mastery of modern design systems.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable 'DataCard' component and a 'UserStats' dashboard widget using Shadcn UI. Ensure they are accessible and mobile-responsive.

steps.title: Wire up Server-Side Logic
steps.description: Connect your frontend to backend services. A portfolio isn't just looks; it's about showing you understand API routes and server-side logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure API route for handling user subscription status updates using Stripe webhooks. Implement error handling and logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "npm run build && npm run lint"

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: A live link is worth a thousand GitHub commits. Deploy your project to Vercel so potential clients can interact with your work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file with environment variables for database URLs and authentication secrets.

pro_tips: Always include a 'Documentation' tab in your app; clients value engineers who write clear docs as much as those who write code.
Use mock data factories to make your dashboard look populated and vibrant, which is essential for that 'first impression' wow factor.
Audit your bundle size early; showing you care about performance metrics separates you from 99% of other freelancers.
Implement a dark mode toggle; it’s a quick win that adds immediate perceived value to your UI design skills.
Keep your git commit history clean and descriptive; clients often check your commit habits to see if you are a professional collaborator.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, record a 60-second Loom video walkthrough of the features and pin it to your portfolio site to highlight your technical communication skills.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect client and start building the perfect engine. Your portfolio is your leverage, and your speed is your currency. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
